summary: Continuous-value input family with a native range slider atom, a canonical SliderField path, and thin purpose-slider wrappers.
philosophy: Teach SliderField as the canonical labeled range-control surface and keep raw Slider as the low-level escape hatch.
Keep native range semantics, numeric normalization, and current-value clamping in core so React and Vue stay aligned.
Treat tooltip and touch-area affordances as visual layers that must not replace visible labeling or ariaValueText.
Keep purpose-slider wrappers thin and semantic so they add meaning without becoming a second slider implementation.

useWhen: The UI needs a continuous or stepped numeric control with visible labeling, optional description, and optional error support.
The product flow maps clearly to VolumeSlider, BrightnessSlider, or RadiusSlider and benefits from self-documenting purpose metadata.
The team wants React and Vue slider behavior to stay aligned through one native-first core recipe and one shared contract file.
avoidWhen: A different control such as radio, segmented control, tabs, or direct numeric input is the more honest interaction model.
The implementation cannot own visible labeling or described-by wiring while using the raw Slider escape hatch.
The team expects dual-thumb ranges, non-native slider semantics, or richer value presentation that this family does not currently define.

automationCovers: Shared slider contract coverage across React and Vue for native range metadata, controlled and uncontrolled value behavior, disabled semantics, tooltip display, and SliderField wiring.
Local adapter tests for raw slider behavior and purpose-wrapper metadata in both frameworks.
Storybook introduction and taxonomy tests in both apps.
manualReviewRequired: Real browser and assistive-technology validation for announcement quality, focus feel, and native range interaction expectations.
Future decisions about which slider stories should join stricter automated accessibility gates.
Whether future keyboard-proof coverage should go beyond the current native smoke-contract boundary.
notes: Slider completed a first-pass audit and now has an explicit spec-backed family contract.
The family still sits outside the wave-1 central semantic registry even though the atom and purpose wrappers already emit useful local metadata.

openQuestions: Which Slider-family stories should later join automated accessibility gates?
Should future slider keyboard-matrix proof move beyond native smoke coverage, or is the native range baseline enough for the supported family contract?
